_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning about the places where we live, why they are special to us, what we can see there, and how we can look after them.

_source.w: We are learning about the places where we live, why they are special to us, what we can see there, and how we can look after them.
y: When we understand our own place, we feel connected and know it is worth caring for.
Knowing what makes a place special helps us respect the places others call home too.
Learning to look after places now means we are helping keep them beautiful for everyone.
eg: Drawing a picture of your street and labelling the park, the shops, and your home.
Talking about why your backyard or a local creek is a special place for you.
Picking up litter at the park to help look after a place we all share.
Listening to an Elder explain why a place is special to their community.
Comparing your neighbourhood with a friend's — noticing what is the same and different.
Planting a small garden at school to care for our shared place.
